steps.title: Apply zero product property
steps.content:
Since 52 is nonzero, the product equals zero when at least one factor is zero:
$$x^3 + 64 = 0 \quad \text{or} \quad x^4 - 81 = 0$$

steps.title: Solve the cubic equation
steps.content: From $x^3 + 64 = 0$, we get $x^3 = -64$. The unique real cube root is $x = -4$. This contributes exactly one real solution.

steps.title: Solve the quartic equation and count total solutions
steps.content:
From $x^4 - 81 = 0$, we get $x^4 = 81 = 3^4$. Both $x = 3$ and $x = -3$ are fourth roots of 81, giving exactly two real solutions.

The three distinct real solutions overall are $x \in \{-4, -3, 3\}$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
